test_rationale.name: happy_path_standard_meeting
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill correctly processes typical meeting notes with clear structure, explicit owners, and concrete deadlines.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: End-to-end processing of all 7 phases with standard inputs; validates that all required output fields are populated correctly.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if any phase fails to execute or if output schema is incorrect; catches missing fields, incorrect date formatting, or wrong owner assignments.
test_rationale.criticality: Critical - this is the baseline happy path that must work for the skill to be usable.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_with_implicit_deadlines
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill can infer implicit deadlines (before next meeting) and handle relative timeframes correctly.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 4 deadline inference logic, especially implicit deadline calculation; Phase 5 reminder scheduling with custom reminder days.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if implicit deadlines are not calculated correctly or if relative timeframes are not converted to ISO 8601 format.
test_rationale.criticality: High - implicit deadline handling is a key feature that distinguishes this skill from simple extraction tools.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_empty_attendee_list
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill can extract attendee names from notes when attendee_list is not provided, and correctly documents this assumption.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 1 attendee extraction logic; error handling for missing attendee_list input; processing_notes documentation.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if attendee extraction fails or if extracted names are not correctly matched to action items.
test_rationale.criticality: High - attendee extraction is a documented feature that enables the skill to work without pre-provided attendee lists.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_vague_deadlines
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that vague deadline language (soon, ASAP, later) is correctly flagged as ambiguous and priority is adjusted accordingly.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 4 vague deadline detection; Phase 6 ambiguity flagging and priority inference; reminder_date null handling for ambiguous deadlines.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if vague deadlines are not flagged, if reminder_date is incorrectly calculated for ambiguous deadlines, or if priority is not set to requires_clarification.
test_rationale.criticality: High - ambiguity detection is a core feature that prevents false confidence in action item deadlines.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_single_action_item
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill correctly handles minimal valid input (single action item) and produces correct output structure.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Minimal input handling; output schema validation with single item; ambiguities_summary structure with single item.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if output structure assumes multiple items or if single-item edge case causes off-by-one errors.
test_rationale.criticality: Medium - edge case that validates robustness but less likely to occur in real usage.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_missing_owner
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that collective assignments (we, team) and missing owners are correctly flagged and handled.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 3 owner assignment rules for collective references; Phase 6 missing_owner flag; priority inference for items with multiple ambiguities.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if collective assignments are not flagged as ambiguous, if unassigned items are not marked, or if priority is not adjusted.
test_rationale.criticality: High - missing owner is a critical ambiguity that must be flagged to prevent action items from being lost.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_owner_not_in_attendee_list
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that owner names not in the attendee list are flagged as unclear_owner and still assigned to the stated name.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 3 attendee list matching logic; Phase 6 unclear_owner flag; error handling for name mismatches.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if names not in attendee list are rejected or if unclear_owner flag is not set.
test_rationale.criticality: High - unclear owner is a common ambiguity that must be flagged to enable follow-up verification.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_past_deadline
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that deadlines in the past are correctly flagged and priority is adjusted to requires_clarification.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 4 past deadline detection; Phase 5 reminder_date null handling for past deadlines; Phase 6 past_deadline flag and priority inference.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if past deadlines are not detected, if reminder_date is incorrectly calculated, or if priority is not adjusted.
test_rationale.criticality: High - past deadlines indicate data quality issues that must be flagged to prevent confusion.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_high_priority_detection
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill correctly identifies high-priority items based on urgent language and short deadlines.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 6 priority inference logic for high-priority items; detection of critical language and blocking dependencies.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if urgent language is not recognized or if high-priority items are assigned lower priority.
test_rationale.criticality: Medium - priority inference is important for action item triage but less critical than ambiguity detection.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_ambiguities_summary_accuracy
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that ambiguities_summary correctly aggregates all flagged items and provides accurate counts by type.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 6 ambiguity compilation and Phase 7 ambiguities_summary generation; accuracy of total_flagged_items, by_type counts, and items_requiring_clarification list.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if ambiguities_summary counts do not match actual flags, if items_requiring_clarification list is incomplete, or if by_type breakdown is inaccurate.
test_rationale.criticality: Critical - ambiguities_summary is a key output that users rely on to understand data quality and required follow-up.

test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if vague deadlines are not flagged, if reminder_date is incorrectly calculated for ambiguous deadlines, or if priority is not set to requires_clarification.
test_rationale.criticality: High - ambiguity detection is a core feature that prevents false confidence in action item deadlines.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_single_action_item
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill correctly handles minimal valid input (single action item) and produces correct output structure.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Minimal input handling; output schema validation with single item; ambiguities_summary structure with single item.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if output structure assumes multiple items or if single-item edge case causes off-by-one errors.
test_rationale.criticality: Medium - edge case that validates robustness but less likely to occur in real usage.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_missing_owner
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that collective assignments (we, team) and missing owners are correctly flagged and handled.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 3 owner assignment rules for collective references; Phase 6 missing_owner flag; priority inference for items with multiple ambiguities.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if collective assignments are not flagged as ambiguous, if unassigned items are not marked, or if priority is not adjusted.
test_rationale.criticality: High - missing owner is a critical ambiguity that must be flagged to prevent action items from being lost.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_owner_not_in_attendee_list
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that owner names not in the attendee list are flagged as unclear_owner and still assigned to the stated name.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 3 attendee list matching logic; Phase 6 unclear_owner flag; error handling for name mismatches.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if names not in attendee list are rejected or if unclear_owner flag is not set.
test_rationale.criticality: High - unclear owner is a common ambiguity that must be flagged to enable follow-up verification.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_past_deadline
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that deadlines in the past are correctly flagged and priority is adjusted to requires_clarification.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 4 past deadline detection; Phase 5 reminder_date null handling for past deadlines; Phase 6 past_deadline flag and priority inference.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if past deadlines are not detected, if reminder_date is incorrectly calculated, or if priority is not adjusted.
test_rationale.criticality: High - past deadlines indicate data quality issues that must be flagged to prevent confusion.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_high_priority_detection
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill correctly identifies high-priority items based on urgent language and short deadlines.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 6 priority inference logic for high-priority items; detection of critical language and blocking dependencies.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if urgent language is not recognized or if high-priority items are assigned lower priority.
test_rationale.criticality: Medium - priority inference is important for action item triage but less critical than ambiguity detection.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_ambiguities_summary_accuracy
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that ambiguities_summary correctly aggregates all flagged items and provides accurate counts by type.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Phase 6 ambiguity compilation and Phase 7 ambiguities_summary generation; accuracy of total_flagged_items, by_type counts, and items_requiring_clarification list.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Breaks if ambiguities_summary counts do not match actual flags, if items_requiring_clarification list is incomplete, or if by_type breakdown is inaccurate.
test_rationale.criticality: Critical - ambiguities_summary is a key output that users rely on to understand data quality and required follow-up.

chain_definition.name: meeting-notes-to-action-items
chain_definition.description: Convert meeting notes into structured action items with owners, deadlines, reminders, and ambiguity flags for review and follow-up.

steps.skill_name: Format and Output Structured Action Items

entry_inputs.meeting_notes: string (required) - raw meeting notes in any text format
entry_inputs.attendee_list: array of strings (optional) - meeting attendee names
entry_inputs.default_reminder_days_before: number (optional, default: 1) - days before deadline to trigger reminder
entry_inputs.timezone: string (optional, default: UTC) - IANA timezone identifier for deadline conversion

terminal_outputs.action_items: array of objects - structured action items with id, description, owner, deadline, reminder_date, priority, ambiguity_flags
terminal_outputs.ambiguities_summary: object - aggregated ambiguity data with total_flagged_items, by_type breakdown, items_requiring_clarification
terminal_outputs.processing_notes: string - summary of processing decisions, assumptions, and ambiguity resolution

goal_requirements.requirement: Parse and validate meeting notes for readability and action-oriented content
Extract all action items from meeting notes using action verb detection
Assign owners to each action item with ambiguity flagging
Extract and infer deadlines with timezone conversion
Calculate reminder dates based on deadlines and preferences
Flag all ambiguities and assess priority levels
Format output as structured JSON with all required fields
Provide ambiguity summary and processing notes for follow-up

execution_plan.description: Sequential pipeline with 7 phases. Each phase depends on output from previous phase. No parallel execution possible due to strict data dependencies.

layers.description: Parse and validate meeting notes, extract attendee list
Extract candidate action items using action verb detection
Assign owners to action items with ambiguity flagging
Extract and infer deadlines with timezone conversion
Determine reminder schedule based on deadlines
Flag ambiguities and assess priority levels
Format and output structured action items with summaries

execution_plan.critical_path: step_1 → step_2 → step_3 → step_4 → step_5 → step_6 → step_7
execution_plan.parallelism_diagram:
Layer 1: [step_1]
  ↓
Layer 2: [step_2]
  ↓
Layer 3: [step_3]
  ↓
Layer 4: [step_4]
  ↓
Layer 5: [step_5]
  ↓
Layer 6: [step_6]
  ↓
Layer 7: [step_7]

No parallel execution possible. All steps are sequentially dependent.
execution_plan.optimization_notes: This chain is optimized for correctness and completeness. Each phase builds on previous outputs with strict data dependencies. No redundancy detected. No skills can be merged without losing functionality. The sequential nature is necessary because: (1) Parsing must precede extraction, (2) Extraction must precede owner assignment, (3) Owner assignment must precede deadline extraction, (4) Deadlines must precede reminder calculation, (5) All data must be complete before priority assessment, (6) Priority assessment must precede final formatting.
